 {"id":"bd-21hz","title":"Widget API Frame Integration Tests","description":"## Purpose

Create comprehensive integration tests for the Widget + Frame API to ensure all Frame features work correctly.

## Test Categories

### 1. Frame Feature Tests
- `frame.buffer` access from Widget::render
- `frame.hit_grid` registration and retrieval
- `frame.cursor_position` setting and clearing
- `frame.degradation` level checking

### 2. Widget Category Tests
Each category should have tests:

**Category A (buffer-only):**
- Block renders borders correctly
- Paragraph wraps text
- Rule draws lines

**Category B (hit testing):**
- List registers hit regions for each item
- Table registers hit regions for rows/cells
- Hit IDs are unique and retrievable

**Category C (cursor):**
- TextInput positions cursor correctly
- Cursor visibility toggles work

**Category D (degradation):**
- ProgressBar simplifies at EssentialOnly
- Spinner shows label-only at reduced levels

### 3. Edge Cases
- Empty area (0x0)
- Single cell area (1x1)
- Unicode content with Frame
- Widget nesting with Frame forwarding

## Test Template

```rust
#[test]
fn test_widget_frame_feature() {
    let mut pool = GraphemePool::new();
    let mut frame = Frame::new(80, 24, &mut pool);
    
    // Arrange
    let widget = TestWidget::new();
    let area = Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5);
    
    // Act
    widget.render(area, &mut frame);
    
    // Assert
    assert!(frame.buffer.get(0, 0).symbol == "expected");
    // Add detailed assertions...
}
```

## Success Criteria
- All Frame features have dedicated tests
- Each widget category has test coverage
- Edge cases documented and tested
- Tests include logging for debugging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:36:31.522608704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T23:44:43.884308586Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T23:44:43.884289249Z","close_reason":"Added Frame integration tests for Frame + widget features","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-3hsz","title":"Text Effects: Shadow and Multi-Layer Rendering","description":"## Background
Flat text lacks depth. Shadows and multi-layer rendering add dimension, improve readability on complex backgrounds, and create professional polish.

## Implementation

Add Shadow struct:
```rust
pub struct Shadow {
    pub dx: i8,
    pub dy: i8,
    pub color: PackedRgba,
    pub opacity: f64,
}
```

Add GlowConfig for multi-layer glow:
```rust
pub struct GlowConfig {
    pub color: PackedRgba,
    pub intensity: f64,    // 0.0-1.0
    pub layers: u8,        // Number of glow layers (1-5)
    pub falloff: f64,      // How quickly glow fades (0.5 = 50% per layer)
}
```

Add to StyledText:
- .shadow(Shadow) - single drop shadow
- .shadows(Vec<Shadow>) - multiple shadow layers
- .glow(GlowConfig) - multi-layer soft glow

NOTE: Outline/stroke is in separate bd-882b bead to keep concerns separate.

Rendering approach:
1. Render shadow/glow layers first (furthest from text first)
2. Each layer offset by (dx*layer, dy*layer) with decreasing opacity
3. Render main text on top
4. Handle edge cases: shadows near terminal bounds use saturating arithmetic

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)
- test_shadow_offset_applied: Shadow appears at dx,dy from text
- test_shadow_opacity: Shadow color correctly alpha-blended
- test_shadow_multiple_layers: Vec<Shadow> renders all layers
- test_glow_layer_count: Correct number of glow layers rendered
- test_glow_falloff: Each layer dimmer by falloff factor
- test_shadow_at_edge: No panic at terminal boundaries
- test_shadow_z_order: Main text renders on top of all shadows
- test_glow_intensity_scales: Higher intensity = brighter glow

## Visual Tests
- Render 'SHADOW' with drop shadow (1,1), verify offset
- Render 'GLOW' with 3-layer glow, verify falloff pattern
- Render at terminal edge, verify no overflow

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Shadow and Multi-Layer Rendering
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-3hy","title":"Define per-module unit test coverage requirements","description":"# Testing: Unit Test Coverage Matrix (per crate/module)

## Purpose
This bead defines **explicit, per-crate unit test expectations** so we can enforce correctness while the codebase grows.

This is intentionally opinionated: the kernel is the product, so kernel crates have higher coverage requirements.

## How To Use
- When adding a new module, add it to the matrix.
- When adding a new public API, add explicit unit tests here.
- CI should enforce these thresholds (see bd-xn2).

---

## Coverage Targets (v1)
These are *minimum* targets. For kernel-critical modules, we should aim higher.

- ftui-render: ≥ 85% (kernel)
- ftui-core: ≥ 80% (terminal/session + input)
- ftui-style: ≥ 80%
- ftui-text: ≥ 80%
- ftui-layout: ≥ 75%
- ftui-runtime: ≥ 75%
- ftui-widgets: ≥ 70%
- ftui-extras: ≥ 60% (feature-gated; correctness still required, but scope is optional)

Note: Some integration-heavy paths (PTY tests) will not show up as unit coverage; they are enforced separately.

---

## ftui-render (≥ 85%)
Kernel correctness lives here.

### Cell / CellContent / CellAttrs
- [ ] CellContent creation from char vs grapheme-id
- [ ] Width semantics (ASCII, wide, combining, emoji)
- [ ] Continuation-cell sentinel semantics for wide glyphs
- [ ] PackedRgba: construction + Porter-Duff alpha blending
- [ ] CellAttrs: bitflags operations + merge/override
- [ ] 16-byte Cell layout invariants (size/alignment) + bits_eq correctness

### Buffer
- [ ] Create/resize buffer with dimensions
- [ ] get/set bounds checking + deterministic defaults
- [ ] Clear semantics (full vs region)
- [ ] Scissor stack push/pop semantics (intersection monotonicity)
- [ ] Opacity stack push/pop semantics (product in [0,1])
- [ ] Wide glyph placement + continuation cells
- [ ] Iteration order and row-major storage assumptions

### Diff
- [ ] empty diff (no changes)
- [ ] single cell change
- [ ] row changes
- [ ] run grouping behavior
- [ ] scratch buffer reuse (no unbounded allocations)

### Presenter
- [ ] cursor tracking correctness
- [ ] style tracking correctness
- [ ] link tracking correctness (OSC 8 open/close)
- [ ] single-write-per-frame behavior
- [ ] synchronized output behavior where supported (fallback correctness)

---

## ftui-core (≥ 80%)

### Event types
- [ ] canonical key/mouse/resize/paste/focus event types are stable

### InputParser
- [ ] bounded CSI/OSC/DCS parsing (DoS limits)
- [ ] bracketed paste decoding + max size
- [ ] mouse SGR decoding
- [ ] focus/resize event decoding

### TerminalCapabilities
- [ ] env heuristic detection (TERM/COLORTERM)
- [ ] mux flags (tmux/screen/zellij) correctness

### TerminalSession lifecycle
- [ ] RAII enter/exit discipline
- [ ] panic cleanup paths are idempotent

---

## ftui-style (≥ 80%)
- [ ] Style defaults + builder ergonomics
- [ ] deterministic style merge (explicit masks)
- [ ] Color downgrade (truecolor→256→16→mono)
- [ ] Theme presets + semantic slots
- [ ] StyleSheet registry + named style composition

---

## ftui-text (≥ 80%)
- [ ] Segment system correctness (Cow<str>)
- [ ] width measurement correctness + LRU cache behavior
- [ ] grapheme segmentation helpers for wrap/truncate correctness
- [ ] wrap/truncate semantics for ZWJ/emoji/combining
- [ ] markup parser correctness (feature-gated)

---

## ftui-layout (≥ 75%)
- [ ] Rect operations (intersection/contains)
- [ ] Flex constraint solving + gaps
- [ ] Grid placement + spanning + named areas
- [ ] Measurement algebra (min/max + clamp)

---

## ftui-runtime (≥ 75%)
- [ ] Program/Model/Cmd lifecycle + scheduler
- [ ] determinism invariants (simulator)
- [ ] subscriptions/ticks integration

---

## ftui-widgets (≥ 70%)
Harness essentials must be tested.

- [ ] Panel/Block borders + title placement
- [ ] Paragraph wrapping + alignment
- [ ] Table sizing + selection
- [ ] Viewport/log viewer scrolling
- [ ] TextInput cursor + edit behavior
- [ ] Progress/spinner rendering

---

## Test Quality Requirements
- Each test is deterministic (no wall-clock timing).
- Each test is fast (target <10ms).
- Failures must be actionable (expected vs actual grid/segments, not “assertion failed”).

## Property Testing
Use property tests for:
- diff correctness
- presenter roundtrip via terminal model
- text width/wrap invariants
- layout solver invariants

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The matrix matches the actual crate boundaries (no stale mapping).
- [ ] CI encodes these thresholds (or a deliberate waiver is documented).
- [ ] Adding a new module requires adding it to this matrix.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:29:14.532567931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:36:01.816690672Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:36:01.816665214Z","close_reason":"Expanded coverage matrix with per-module expectations","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3hy","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":428,"issue_id":"bd-3hy","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: Reopening per user request; keep issues open until explicitly agreed to close.","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:18:15Z"}]}

Extend set_fast to also fast-path bg alpha 0 by preserving the existing cell background (equivalent to src-over with alpha 0). Add unit tests in buffer.rs and extend buffer_bench to cover set_fast with transparent bg.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SapphireGorge","created_at":"2026-02-10T07:11:06.646546329Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T07:16:37.466586674Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T07:16:37.466559714Z","close_reason":"Buffer::set_fast now fast-paths bg alpha 0 (preserve existing bg) and 255; added tests + buffer_bench coverage; fmt/check/clippy clean; cargo test -p ftui-render.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["perf","render"]}
 {"id":"bd-3i6","title":"Implement bracketed paste and mouse SGR protocol","description":"PHASE 3 - ADVANCED INPUT PROTOCOLS

## Background
Bracketed paste mode prevents pasted content from being interpreted as commands. SGR mouse protocol provides precise mouse reporting with modifiers.

## Technical Requirements

### Bracketed Paste Mode
Escape sequences:
- Enable: ESC[?2004h
- Disable: ESC[?2004l
- Paste start: ESC[200~
- Paste end: ESC[201~

pub struct BracketedPasteParser {
    in_paste: bool,
    paste_buffer: Vec<u8>,
    max_paste_size: usize,  // Default: 1MB
}

impl BracketedPasteParser {
    pub fn parse(&mut self, byte: u8) -> Option<InputEvent> {
        if self.in_paste {
            // Accumulate until paste end sequence
            // Enforce max_paste_size limit
        } else {
            // Check for paste start sequence
        }
    }
}

pub enum InputEvent {
    Key(KeyEvent),
    Mouse(MouseEvent),
    Paste(String),  // Complete paste content
    Resize(u16, u16),
    Focus(bool),
}

### SGR Mouse Protocol (1006)
Escape sequence: ESC[<Cb;Cx;CyM or ESC[<Cb;Cx;Cym

struct SgrMouseParser;

impl SgrMouseParser {
    /// Parse SGR extended mouse report
    pub fn parse(&self, params: &[u8]) -> Option<MouseEvent> {
        // Parse: <button;column;rowM or <button;column;rowm
        // Button encoding:
        //   0 = left, 1 = middle, 2 = right
        //   +4 = shift, +8 = meta, +16 = ctrl
        //   32 = motion, 64 = scroll
        // M = press, m = release
    }
}

pub struct MouseEvent {
    pub kind: MouseEventKind,
    pub column: u16,
    pub row: u16,
    pub modifiers: KeyModifiers,
}

pub enum MouseEventKind {
    Down(MouseButton),
    Up(MouseButton),
    Drag(MouseButton),
    Moved,
    ScrollDown,
    ScrollUp,
    ScrollLeft,
    ScrollRight,
}

### Enable/Disable Mouse
pub fn enable_mouse_capture(writer: &mut impl Write) -> io::Result<()> {
    // Enable SGR extended mode (1006)
    // Enable any-event tracking (1003) or button-event (1002)
    write!(writer, "\x1b[?1000h\x1b[?1006h")?;
    Ok(())
}

pub fn disable_mouse_capture(writer: &mut impl Write) -> io::Result<()> {
    write!(writer, "\x1b[?1006l\x1b[?1000l")?;
    Ok(())
}

### Paste Size Limits
- Default max: 1MB
- Configurable via builder
- Large pastes: return error or truncate

### Focus Events (bonus)
- Enable: ESC[?1004h
- Disable: ESC[?1004l
- Focus in: ESC[I
- Focus out: ESC[O

## Tests Required
- [ ] Bracketed paste parse correct
- [ ] Paste size limit enforced
- [ ] SGR mouse button decoding
- [ ] SGR mouse position decoding
- [ ] Modifier key detection
- [ ] Scroll events
- [ ] Motion events
- [ ] Focus events

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bracketed paste with size limit
- [ ] SGR mouse protocol parsing
- [ ] All mouse event types
- [ ] Modifier key capture
- [ ] Focus in/out events
- [ ] Enable/disable APIs

Considerations: Avoid global time dependence; use explicit time steps and seeded RNGs; document how to reproduce outputs; provide a stable hash registry key format (mode+size+seed).","acceptance_criteria":"1) Deterministic fixture utilities exist for seeds, time steps, and env capture.\n2) E2E scripts and demo tests use fixtures to produce stable hashes.\n3) Reproduction steps documented for CI and local runs.\n4) Fixture utilities include deterministic unit tests with rich assertion context (no mocks).","notes":"Progress 2026-02-04: VFX harness JSONL now includes timestamp + hash_key (start + frame lines) via determinism helpers. cargo check failed due to existing ftui-extras/mermaid parse_directive_* unresolved symbols.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"codex","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:22:02.973432455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:12:21.531367973Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T21:02:24.181726137Z","close_reason":"Determinism fixtures + tests added; demo/E2E logs now use fixture timestamps/run_id; docs updated","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3isi7","depends_on_id":"bd-2txo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:23:11.311536036Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
